concept: Include / Bring Within / Make Part Of
description: To bring someone or something within a group, category, or whole, making them part of what was previously bounded without them. 'Pharune' names the act of expanding belonging—widening the circle to embrace what was outside.
sound_symbolism: The word expands to encompass. 'Pha' /ɸa/ begins with breathy openness, boundary becoming permeable. Then 'ru' rolls with gathering warmth, bringing in. Finally 'ne' settles with nasal security, the included one now safely within.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi ru pharune' (I include you). Can specify group: 'mi ru mua lo nesi pharune' (I include you in the family). Opposite of exclusion—pharune affirms belonging.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Inclusion practices expand the 'we'—the more we include, the fewer 'others' remain outside our circle of care.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Inclusive communities are resilient communities—diversity of members strengthens the whole.
